summary: POD GGUF BLOCKER diagnosed (screenshot, live 5090 Pod): ComfyUI-GGUF node import FAILS on the Pod with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'gguf'`. TWO gaps: (1) the Pod image's node_lock.json (mpi-ci/cubric-vision-pod/node_lock.json) is a STALE COPY — it does NOT have the ComfyUI-GGUF entry I added to the app's dev_configs/node_lock.json, so the image build never installed the node OR its gguf pip dep (per the bump-rebuild law: node_lock edit = rebuild BOTH images). (2) The per-model VOLUME-install path (routes/remoteModels.js remoteInstallDep, line 284) only passes install_command to the wrapper when dep.installRequirementsCommand (a CUSTOM command) is set — it SILENTLY DROPS installRequirements:true (the standard requirements.txt flag). ComfyUI-GGUF uses installRequirements:true → so even though the wrapper pushed the node folder to the volume, it never ran `pip install -r requirements.txt` → no gguf pkg → import fails. FIX OPTIONS: (a) add ComfyUI-GGUF to the Pod node_lock.json + rebuild the Pod image (bakes node+gguf in, but ComfyUI-GGUF is MODEL-specific not universal — does it belong baked? the image loop installs ALL node_lock nodes regardless of installOnEngine, so adding it there WOULD bake it = acceptable for now); AND/OR (b) FIX remoteModels.js so installRequirements:true also triggers a remote pip install -r requirements.txt (the wrapper must support running requirements.txt for a volume node, not just a custom command) — this is the GENERAL fix so future model-specific nodes with a requirements.txt work remotely. (b) is the real bug. Detail in handoff 60c42614.
